notes: Iteration 9: FULL MODE plan based on iter_008 pilot consolidation (Synthesis Round 5). Paper restructured from THREE to TWO contributions: (1) Cross-domain absorption characterization, (2) Causal mechanism confirmation. GAS, CMI, Absorption Tax demoted to appendix. Three critical gaps: (a) probe quality at multiple layers, (b) activation patching at scale (n>=20), (c) multi-layer multi-SAE absorption measurement. Phase 0 blocking experiments completed in pilot -- scale up for statistical rigor. All inference-only on pre-trained SAEs. GPU: RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (95GB VRAM). Total: ~9 GPU-hours, ~11 wall-clock hours.

tasks.name: Environment Verification
tasks.description: Verify environment is still functional from iter_008. Quick check: SAELens, TransformerLens, GPU, Gemma 2B model, RAVEL, sae_spelling. All confirmed working in iter_008 pilot, so this is a fast sanity check. Output: setup_check.json.

pilot.pass_criteria: All critical packages importable, GPU available, Gemma 2B loadable, RAVEL accessible, sae_spelling importable.

tasks.name: Phase 0.4: Report Threshold Sensitivity Data from iter_001
tasks.description: CPU-only analysis. The ablation_threshold_sensitivity.json (141KB, 5x4 grid: cosine thresholds {0.01, 0.02, 0.025, 0.03, 0.05} x magnitude gaps {0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0}) was computed in iter_001 but NEVER reported. Load, parse, and determine: is the control failure threshold-dependent (fixable) or structural (fundamental to JumpReLU)? Generate summary table for appendix. This was attempted but FAILED in iter_008 -- ensure correct path and parsing this time.

pilot.pass_criteria: 5x4 grid parsed successfully. Summary table with interpretation per cell. Overall conclusion: threshold-dependent vs structural.

visualizations.description: Threshold sensitivity: cosine threshold x magnitude gap x control failure rate

tasks.name: Phase 0.1: Activation Patching at Scale (n>=20 words, 200 contexts)
tasks.description: Scale up the causal absorption evidence from pilot (n=7 words, Wilcoxon p=1.0). MUST achieve statistical significance for paper. Pipeline: (1) Load Gemma 2B + Gemma Scope L12-16k SAE. (2) Identify >= 20 absorption pairs: start with 7 pilot words (eight, liked, lower, offer, often, other, under), then use integrated-gradients on first-letter FN tokens to find additional word-child pairs. (3) For each word x 200 input contexts: (a) Cache SAE activations, (b) Zero child feature, (c) Pass modified activations through remaining layers, (d) Check parent probe recovery. (4) Control: zero random non-child feature (matched activation magnitude) per context. (5) Statistical tests: Wilcoxon signed-rank (target p < 0.01), bootstrap 95% CI on recovery difference. (6) Cross-hierarchy: repeat for city-continent and city-language absorption pairs if probes pass quality gate. (7) Report per-word: absorption rate, recovery rate (child vs control), prob_change.
tasks.comment_on_duration: 20+ words x 200 contexts x (child + control) = ~8000 forward passes. Each forward pass: full model + SAE encode/decode. With batch=8 on 95GB VRAM, ~1000 batches.

pilot.pass_criteria: At least 15 words processed with >= 100 contexts each. Recovery rate computed for child-zeroed and control. Wilcoxon p < 0.05 (relaxed pilot gate). If still p > 0.05 with n=15+: the effect may be too weak for statistical significance -- report with exact p-value and effect size.

visualizations.description: Activation patching per word: recovery rate (child vs control)
Recovery rate distribution: child-zeroed vs. control-zeroed

tasks.name: Phase 4.2: Final Consolidation and Writing Gate
tasks.description: Aggregate all experimental results into a comprehensive summary for writing. Pipeline: (1) Compile probe quality results: which hierarchies pass quality gate at which layers. (2) Compile cross-domain absorption rates with CIs and statistical tests. (3) Compile activation patching: final Wilcoxon p, recovery rate, effect size. (4) Compile tightened hedging per hierarchy. (5) Compile architecture comparison. (6) Compile appendix results: GAS (negative), CMI (negative), Absorption Tax (qualitative). (7) Compile threshold sensitivity from iter_001 data. (8) Update hypothesis verdicts for H1-H7. (9) Writing gate: go_write=true if primary contribution (cross-domain) has statistical evidence. (10) Generate per-paper-section result summaries.

pilot.pass_criteria: consolidation_summary.json with all hypothesis verdicts, writing gate decision, per-section result summaries, and negative results documentation.

parallelization_note: Wave 1: setup + threshold reporting (0 + 1 GPU, 15 min). Wave 2: probe training (1 GPU, 60 min -- CRITICAL PATH). Wave 3: first-letter absorption + activation patching (2 GPUs parallel, 60 min). Wave 4: cross-domain absorption + GAS (2 GPUs, 60 min). Wave 5: hedging + architecture + tax (3 tasks, 2 GPUs, 45 min). Wave 6: consolidation (CPU, 20 min). Total wall-clock: ~5.5 hours with 4 GPUs, ~8 hours with 1 GPU.

fallback_decisions.if_probes_still_below_gate_at_all_layers: Relax quality gate to F1 >= 0.80 with documented caveat. If still failing: restrict to city-continent (6-class, simplest) and city-language (decent pilot F1=0.79). Report probe quality as limitation.
fallback_decisions.if_activation_patching_still_underpowere: If Wilcoxon p > 0.05 even with n >= 20: report with exact p-value and effect size. The 14.3% vs 0.5% difference from pilot is still meaningful directional evidence even without statistical significance at n=20.
fallback_decisions.if_cross_domain_rates_reverse_with_bette: If first-letter absorption becomes >= semantic hierarchies with quality probes: report as H2' falsified. This is still a publishable finding (probe quality matters more than hierarchy type).
fallback_decisions.if_jumprelu_advantage_disappears: If JumpReLU no longer shows lowest absorption with quality probes: report architecture comparison as genuinely inconclusive. The single-task benchmark criticism still stands.
fallback_decisions.if_saebench_saes_not_available: Restrict architecture comparison to JumpReLU across L0/width within Gemma Scope. Document BatchTopK/Matryoshka as future work.
fallback_decisions.if_gas_improves_with_10k_sequences: If rho >= 0.3 with 10k sequences: promote GAS from appendix back to main text. However, pilot CI [-0.26, 0.52] suggests this is unlikely.

paper_framing.contribution_1: First cross-domain absorption characterization: semantic hierarchies show more absorption than first-letter spelling, reframing the entire absorption literature
paper_framing.contribution_2: Causal mechanism confirmation: activation patching provides first interventional evidence for competitive exclusion; tightened hedging reveals loose classification is near-tautological
appendix_material: Absorption Tax quantitative predictions (not supported, rho=0.08/0.16; qualitative direction reported)
Threshold sensitivity grid from iter_001 (5x4 grid, never previously reported)
honest_negative_results: H2 falsified (first-letter is NOT worst case -- positive finding)

reusable_from_iter_008.code: iter_008/exp/code/ -- all 12 experiment scripts can be adapted to full-mode sample sizes
reusable_from_iter_008.pilot_results: iter_008/exp/results/ -- pilot consolidation provides baseline for full-mode comparisons
reusable_from_iter_008.environment: Confirmed working: gemma-2-2b, SAELens 6.39.0, sae_spelling, RAVEL, RTX PRO 6000
